review_title: Great for Preview Testing Designs on all Devices and ESP
author: Lindsey Raye P.
pros: Great for Preview Testing Designs on all Devices and ESP; you could see how the mobile version looked, the outlook versions, google, yahoo, and unfortunately a lot of html templates in Salesforce are always tricky with outlook.
cons: It was harder to view the exact dimensions needed on images to display correctly

review_title: Great solution highly recommended for email marketers
description: My experience has been really great from a product capabilities, support and reliability standpoint. When my coworkers found something wrong and needed help troubleshooting, it was very easy to get a hold of a customer support person who was able to quickly walk through and successfully solve the issues.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The campaign precheck and deliverability testing are great features that allow you to pinpoint potential issues with a coded email file, to prevent anything from going wrong before launching an email. Its a very good tool for helping to prepare and navigate through the email testing and deploying process.
cons: It is not always easy to find information about the latest product releases and what it would take to add on or try out new features before deciding if we want to add them to our subscription. It would be great to have more visibility into this on a more regular basis.

review_title: Email on acid review
description: How to overall great experience with email on acid I think it is super helpful and definitely worth the money would highly recommend to fellow friends and colleagues
author: Alex A.
pros: I really enjoyed this tool very easy to use. Great customer support wonderful email management and test management. Super helpful for email market. And worse, it’s weight in gold.
cons: How to overall good experience with email on acid to pick one certain thing, I wasn’t a big fan of it would definitely have to be atuomation
author_profession: Pure support specialist, drug and alcohol counselor
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Functional, but a long way behind the competition
author: Kay S.
pros: The main attraction that Email on Acid held for my business was the lower cost, in comparison to the main competitor in the field, Litmus, both as an email editor and for screenshot testing of emails in different email clients. Email on Acid has a wide range of supported clients, including some that Litmus does not include.
cons: Email on Acid has a wide range of supported clents for screenshot testing, but this coverage is distinctly lacking in several key areas, notably dark mode in Gmail on Android/iOS.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Email on Acid - Great Product with Unlimited Tests!
description: I build emails every day and am able to build out custom emails very quickly and that render across most platforms!
author: Mike W.
pros: One of the things I love most about Email on Acid is you can run unlimited number of tests across desktop, mobile and tablet size devices. I switched away from Litmus to Email on Acid and with unlimited tests along with all of the other great features, I'm never going back!
cons: In the Design Editor, the hyperlinks don't link out into a new window which would be a nice feature to have to make sure the target urls are correct.

review_title: The best email testing tool in the market.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Capturing different domain screenshot, that was absolutely a great deal since before we had to create different test email accounts across platforms. You can even customize the favorite list of domains, platforms, OS etc for further testing based on market trends.
cons: Cost would be the one con factor to point out. Have also used Litmus extensively and has found that its the same there as well.

review_title: Long time user, and the evolution of Email on Acid has been great!
author: William T.
pros: I've been using Email on Acid for about 6 years, and its managed to evolve, adapt, and stay relevant as HTML email gets more complicated in a more responsive world. There is almost no wait-time between when I deploy a test email to Email on Acid and when I can start viewing renderings. They also have great articles about HTML email trends, useful snippets for those hard to fix bugs (I'm looking at you Outlook).
cons: Their latest (within the last month or two of this article) UI refresh is a little wonky. I'm sure they'll have the kinks out by the time you read this.

review_title: Very helpful for end user experience
author: Brittany C.
pros: It is easy to upload your email creative to see how it will be viewed on different devices and browsers, helped us improve our design.
cons: There really isn't anything I do not like. Maybe receiving their informational emails? lol

review_title: Cheap, but slightly limited, email tester
description: It helped us to quickly test our emails in a wide range of email clients, something that is almost impossible to do manually. It helped us find a number of bugs in our emails and in our styling and the styling coach helped us to quickly identify the attribute that was causing the issues.
author: Dylan E.
pros: It is relatively cheap (cheaper than most competitors), while still having a wide range of email clients to test in. There is a free trial that is more than sufficient to try out all the main functionalities of the application, so you can try it out (and even update some emails) without any costs. The email styling coach was surprisingly useful, since all hints were very concrete and lists the offending line of code.
cons: The list of email client, although extensive, does not have the latest versions of some popular clients (during my work with the application it was missing the latest IBM notes and therefore could not reproduce a known bug).

review_title: Email testing done right
description: When creating a new email, it is important that is looks right to all users. We use Email on Acid to make sure we achieve this.
author: Ramona E.
pros: This cloud-based application allows you to 'test' emails in a number of different email clients. Anyone who has worked on emails knows that email clients are notoriously bad at implementing all features, which means that often your email will look great on one email client, but crappy on another (because you need to use some client-specific attribute). This application allows you to enter HTML and see how it looks in various email clients. It even gives code suggestions to help make the email look uniform over all clients.
cons: The only minor downside I have found is that some older (but still in use) email clients are not present.

review_title: Worth it, if you can afford it.
description: Overall, I loved the product and what it offered, but the pricing was too steep to maintain for my small business.
author: Tammy W.
pros: I love the fact that Email on Acid can test against spam filters so that I can change things to improve chances of delivery.
cons: Pricing is too steep for my small business to maintain.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Fail proof your emails on all devices in a single click
description: Overall the best out there for responsiveness test, spam score and much more, easy to use, deploy and integrate as well
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: How simple it is to simply drop your HTML template and get a nice and detailed preview of that on multiple devices
cons: Could use a revamp on the availability of devices for some niche modern smartphones

review_title: Email on Acid is a Must Have
description: Email on Acid is a must for me since I am an email marketer. When using templates over and over and changing the code, errors can happen and I fully trust Email on Acid to tell me if my email if my email is broken or not rendering properly. I will not send an email until I run it through here first.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I love about Email on Acid is how quickly I can see how my email renders across all email providers as well as internet browsers. I always rely on this tool to make sure my emails will be sent out with no issues. The one cool thing that I really do love is that I can use their optimizer to check my HTML code. Since I am not fully fluent in coding, I find myself using existing templates and making modifications to the code. Many times I notice an error in the code and am not sure what is broken or wrong, but when I take the code and run it through Email on Acid's code optimizer, it tells me the errors and fixes them.
cons: I have no complaints about Email on Acid. I have always had a great experience with it.

review_title: Avis utiles que E-mail on Acid
description: Elle a permis à de nombreuses personnes dans diverse domaine de gérer plusieurs business notamment dans l’informatique
author: Godwill O.
pros: L’option qui permet aux utilisateurs de suivre les mail
cons: Particulièrement je n’ai pas trouvé une option non appréciante sur cette app

review_title: Email On Acid Review
description: My overall experience with Email on Acid is that I use it for a variety of clients. I create a large amount of emails and need to save time with a testing software. Email on acid allows me to quickly and efficiently do this. I can quickly spot issues with images, coloring or spacing and identify which specific email platforms it has issues on.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I personally love email on acid. As a consultant who creates a variety of emails, email templates and assets within those emails and links, it is crucial that I can quickly and accurately test how all these links and images are rendering on different browsers or email softwares. Email on Acid allows me to very quickly upload emails that I have in draft form from a variety of marketing platforms. It allows me to quickly test and spot and identity any potential issues with images within the email. It also helps me identify any issues with spacing or coloring. Email on Acid saves me a lot of time and mistakes that could potentially end up being client facing.
cons: I personally do not have any cons with using Email on Acid to test my newly created emails. I have not used any other testing softwares to compare but personally love Email on Acid.

review_title: So Important to Preview Your Emails
description: When I need to update or create an email template, I need Email on Acid to be sure it looks how I want it to look
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is so easy to upload an html file and see it rendered in all the different email clients. It's also very helpful to be able to see what the email client is actually rendering to compare with what you expect it to render
cons: It would be nice if your email tests didn't get saved automatically. It would also be nice to exclude some email clients from testing if desired

review_title: Email on Acid is a great tool for email marketers
description: Email on Acid has been a simple and easy tool to use for our company's email testing. It makes identifying coding issues a breeze. The interface is very visual and easy-to-use, as you can easily identify the differences in rendering right on the spot. EOA has saved our butts on a few different occasions :)
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Email on Acid makes email render testing a breeze. By sending a test of your email to your pre-determined EOA email address, you can quickly and easily view how your email will display across dozens of browsers, operating systems, devices, etc.
cons: It is not all-encompassing as far as the number of browsers/OS/devices it accounts for. There are a few that are missing, which I'm hoping will be added as time goes on.

review_title: A cheaper alternative that gets the job done
description: If you're a company with an unlimited budget, then I would choose Litmus. But for the rest of us, especially freelancers/contractors, Email on Acid is a great choice that won't let you down. I have peace of mind when everything finally renders appropriately across email clients/devices. Getting there can be a royal pain, but EoA is a major help.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Email screenshots happen almost instantly. You can automatically trigger an email test by sending a test message to your EoA account. You can configure which email clients to include, and share the results.
cons: This still feels a bit like a 'budget' product, compared to Litmus. The price tag definitely reflects that, and boy am I thankful there IS a cheaper option. So I won't complain too much. But I do sometimes get frustrated that certain screenshots take forever, and sometimes they render badly. Small price to pay for a product that's actually in my budget as a freelancer.

review_title: Great tool to check email layouts
description: It's an unnerving feeling to send an email out to thousands of people, and email on acid helps put your mind at ease knowing your emails look great in any viewing platform.
author: Jacob H.
pros: With every user having a different journey, email on acid is a great tool to use to check your HTML coding and layout so your emails look crisp and clean in any viewing platform.
cons: Sometimes it takes a while sent to the email on acid email address a while for it to populate, which makes it a challenge when you want to send an email out immediately.

review_title: Used in two capacities with different clients... user and admin. Easy once you get the hang of it.
author: Rob H.
pros: It was easy to get started. I LOVE being able to just send an email to a "secret" address and get visual results for various email clients for testing. The pricing is fair and straightforward.
cons: Can't seem to figure out how to get help with correcting small code issues or code typos. Would be a nice feature. Perhaps the platform could offer tips based on your html.

review_title: Frequent User
description: I use this software A LOT. It is very intuitive to use and it is helpful in ensuring your emails look great.
author: Daryl H.
pros: I love the idea behind this software so you can see how your emails will appear in multiple clients, but...
cons: I dislike that this software allows you to see your ememo across multiple clients because then project managers get caught up in details in obscure email clients.

review_title: null
author: Ariel D.

review_title: Great Email Preview Tool, But Litmus Is Better
author: Russell C.
pros: Email on acid is a great tool for what it's supposed to do. Gives you previews for all the clients you would possibly need.
cons: It's a fine software but if you're looking for a email preview tool then you should just use litmus.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Email on Acid Review
description: We run all our email blasts through Email on Acid and feel confident our emails will look their best. Email on Acid saves us time so we don't have to do our own testing on every client.
author: Leah C.
pros: Love all the different clients that show how your email will look.
cons: Sometimes it takes awhile for the screen shots of your email to render.

review_title: Wish I Knew About This Sooner
author: Tara H.
pros: This is a great email litmus test to ensure that your email newsletter is going to show up the same way on your preview as on every device imaginable.
cons: Wasn't really good at telling what an issue was.
author_profession: Marketing Coordinator & Corporate Social Responsibility Officer
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
